reasons: Baxia directly reduces the value of Thamuz's sustain-based fighting.
Thamuz wants long brawls, while Baxia makes those brawls less favorable by cutting healing and shield value.

proof.summary: Thamuz relies on extended sustain during his ultimate window, while Baxia reduces healing and shield effects around him.
worksBestWhen: Baxia keeps contact with Thamuz during his sustain window.
Baxia's team follows up instead of letting Thamuz reset.
failureCases: Thamuz avoids Baxia and dives the backline.
Baxia is under-farmed and cannot survive the brawl.

reasons: Dyrroth can reduce Thamuz's defensive value and punish him before the fight becomes a long sustain battle.
The matchup is strongest in lane or small skirmishes where Dyrroth can force short burst trades.

proof.summary: Thamuz wants durable extended trades, while Dyrroth's defense reduction and burst pressure make him vulnerable before sustain fully takes over.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth trades around enhanced skill timing.
Dyrroth forces short trades before Thamuz gets full sustain value.
failureCases: Dyrroth misuses burst before Thamuz activates sustain.
Thamuz drags the fight long enough for Dyrroth's damage window to expire.

reasons: Karrie punishes Thamuz's durability with true damage based on target Max HP.
She is strongest when she can kite Thamuz instead of standing inside his close-range brawl.

proof.summary: Thamuz wants to survive through durability and sustain, while Karrie's true-damage Max HP mechanic pressures him even when he builds tanky.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel and space to keep attacking.
Karrie avoids being forced into melee range.
failureCases: Thamuz reaches Karrie before she stacks damage.
Karrie is isolated without frontline protection.

reasons: Valir can push Thamuz away and slow down his attempt to force an extended melee fight.
Thamuz becomes less threatening when he cannot stay attached to priority targets.

proof.summary: Thamuz wants to walk into sustained melee range, while Valir can knock him back and reset spacing before Thamuz finishes the brawl.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Thamuz's commit.
Valir positions behind frontline instead of trading alone.
failureCases: Valir wastes knockback before Thamuz commits.

reasons: Lunox can punish Thamuz with magic burst while using defensive timing to avoid his strongest brawl window.
She works well when Thamuz overcommits into her damage window.

proof.summary: Thamuz wants to keep enemies inside his sustained fight, while Lunox can use defensive timing to avoid or punish the commit.
worksBestWhen: Lunox holds defensive timing until Thamuz commits.
Teammates help keep Thamuz from sticking to her.
failureCases: Lunox uses defensive timing too early.
Thamuz catches Lunox without escape or peel.
